channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Trump Is Urged to Move on Nuclear Site Thought to Be Beyond Reach of Bombs
item.description: Little is known about Pickaxe Mountain, but some experts say it illustrates the impossibility of relying on force alone to prevent Iran from acquiring a nuclear bomb.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
item.media:description: A satellite image provided by Vantor shows the tunnel complex at Pickaxe Mountain in Iran last month. Last fall, satellite images revealed that Iran had advanced construction at the site soon after U.S. forces disabled the country’s three main nuclear facilities in June.

item.title: Trump Extends Sanctions Exemption on Some Russian Oil as High Gas Prices Persist
item.description: The Trump administration made the announcement hours after Iran said that the Strait of Hormuz was open to commercial ships.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said on Wednesday that the U.S. government would not renew the sanctions exemption on Russian oil already at sea.

item.title: A Potent Threat in Strait of Hormuz: Iran’s “Mosquito Fleet”
item.description: Separate from the regular Iranian Navy, with boats that often go more than 115 miles per hour, it’s what a retired U.S. official calls a “disruptive force.”
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: An Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps speedboat and navy ship in the Persian Gulf, in 2024.

item.title: Federal Court Temporarily Freezes Nexstar’s Merger With Tegna
item.description: The judge said the two television companies could not combine operations while an antitrust lawsuit proceeded. Nexstar said its deal was already done.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Mullin
item.media:description: A federal judge temporarily blocked Nexstar from combining its operations with the station group Tegna.

item.title: Trump’s Dispute With Pope Leo Deepens Divisions on the Right
item.description: Sean Hannity criticized the pope. Tucker Carlson attacked Mr. Hannity. And President Trump suggested ranking MAGA figures: “good, bad, and somewhere in the middle.”
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump has sharply criticized the pope after his comments about the war in Iran.

item.title: Trump Will Participate in a Marathon Bible Reading
item.description: He will read a passage from the Old Testament that his Christian supporters cite as a call to national repentance and divine blessing.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Graham
item.media:description: The authorities dispersed protesters in 2020 so President Trump could have his photo taken with a Bible in front of a Washington church.

item.title: In Angola, Pope Leo XIV Faces the Legacy of Slavery
item.description: His visit includes a trip to a shrine where enslaved Africans were baptized before being forced into the treacherous voyage across the Atlantic Ocean.
item.dc:creator: Cláudio C. Silva and John Eligon
item.media:description: Pope Leo XIV in Cameroon on Thursday. He is scheduled to visit Angola on Saturday as part of his 10-day trip through Africa.

item.title: How Trump Helped Pope Leo Find His Voice
item.description: After his election last year, Leo XIV developed a reputation as a mild-mannered mediator. President Trump’s attacks have made him more combative.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Pope Leo XIV in Cameroon on Friday. His strident speeches in Africa this week have marked a shift in his rhetoric.

item.title: Prosecutor Withdraws From Trump Team’s Investigation of Ex-CIA Director John O. Brennan
item.description: A career Justice Department lawyer, Maria Medetis Long, in Miami is said to have raised concerns about whether the evidence justified moving forward with a bid to prosecute John O. Brennan.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Savage, Alan Feuer and Glenn Thrush
item.category: Russian Interference in 2016 US Elections and Ties to Trump Associates
item.media:description: John O. Brennan during a Senate Intelligence Committee hearing on Jan. 10, 2017, when he was still C.I.A. director, on Russia’s interference in the previous year’s presidential campaign.

item.title: With Vaccines Widely Popular, Kennedy Changes Tone, but Maybe Not His Plans
item.description: Several moves suggest Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. could revive his campaign to question the safety and effectiveness of the shots after the midterm elections.
item.dc:creator: Sheryl Gay Stolberg, Apoorva Mandavilli and Christina Jewett
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:credit: Demetrius Freeman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. testifying before the House Committee on Ways and Means on Thursday.

item.title: White House and Anthropic Hold ‘Productive’ Meeting, Aiming for a Compromise
item.description: Friday’s meeting at the White House followed the introduction of Anthropic’s powerful new artificial intelligence model, Mythos, which U.S. officials believe could be critical for security.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes, Sheera Frenkel and Tyler Pager
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Trump administration is seeking to strike a compromise with Anthropic after the Pentagon named the company a supply chain risk.

item.title: Trump Spat Gives Spain Leader Pedro Sánchez a Political Lifeline
item.description: To leftists abroad, Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez of Spain is a hero for standing up to President Trump. At home, Mr. Trump is seen as Mr. Sánchez’s political savior from thorny domestic challenges.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez of Spain addressing Parliament in March.

item.title: Japan’s Cherry Blossom Database, 1,200 Years Old, Has a New Keeper
item.description: The remarkable catalog of dates is one of the longest-running records of climate change. Its creator died, setting off a search for a successor.
item.dc:creator: Hiroko Tabuchi
item.media:description: Cherry blossoms at Rokkakudo Temple in Kyoto, Japan, recently.

item.title: Carney Courts Investors to Reduce Canada’s Economic Dependence on U.S.
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney has set up a meeting and a new office to speed project approvals and make Canada more attractive to investors.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada on Parliament Hill this week in Ottawa.

item.title: Lutnick Says Canada Trade Deal Needs to Be Reworked Ahead of Talks
item.description: Howard Lutnick, President Trump’s commerce secretary, derided Canada’s trade strategy and said a North American deal needed to be reworked.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson and Matina Stevis-Gridneff
item.media:credit: Yuri Gripas for The New York Times
item.media:description: Howard Lutnick, the U.S. Commerce Secretary, in Washington in February.

item.title: Supreme Court Sides With Oil Companies in Louisiana Coastal Lawsuits
item.description: The companies had asked the justices to clear the way to move environmental lawsuits out of state courts, to friendlier federal venues.
item.dc:creator: Abbie VanSickle
item.media:credit: Bryan Tarnowski for The New York Times
item.media:description: Wetlands along the Mississippi River as it nears the Gulf of Mexico in southeast Louisiana.

item.title: Western States Need Water. San Diego Has Extra. Will They Make A Deal?
item.description: San Diego County is shopping a surplus of desalinated seawater to Western states that are facing increasingly urgent drought and short supplies.
item.dc:creator: Scott Dance
item.media:credit: John Francis Peters for The New York Times
item.media:description: A view of San Diego and the Pacific Ocean from Cabrillo National Monument.

item.title: Rescuers Try to Save Timmy, a Whale Stranded Off Germany
item.description: A month of efforts to help a stranded humpback escape the Baltic Sea have culminated in a tourist and media spectacle, with no guarantee of success.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley
item.media:description: Off Poel Island in northern Germany on Thursday. A humpback whale has been stranded in the area for almost a month.

item.title: Our Tax System Should Make You Furious
item.description: The tax expert Ray Madoff explains why the American tax system is broken, and how to make it fairer.
item.dc:creator: Ezra Klein and Rollin Hu

item.title: A Paris Court Just Rewrote the Rules of Corporate Morality
item.description: The profit motive was on trial. The verdict was scathing.
item.dc:creator: M. Gessen
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
Islamic State in Iraq and Syria (ISIS)
item.media:credit: Photo illustration by The New York Times; photographs by Behrouz Mehri and Delil Souleiman/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Bruno Lafont, the former chief executive of Lafarge.

item.title: Tornados Reported Across the Midwest as Powerful Storms Slam the Region
item.description: One reported tornado downed trees and damaged cars in Lena, Ill., an official said.
item.dc:creator: Judson Jones, Hannah Ziegler and Amy Graff

item.title: Starmer’s Credibility Just Took Another Body Blow Over the Mandelson Scandal
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer appears to have been kept in the dark repeatedly over Peter Mandelson, the Jeffrey Epstein associate — fueling an image of weakness.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer in Paris on Friday. “That I wasn’t told that Peter Mandelson had failed security vetting when he’s appointed is staggering,” he said.

item.title: An Explosion Rattles a Toronto Neighborhood. A Drake Video Was to Blame.
item.description: A blast on Thursday night turned out to be for a video shoot, but it unnerved residents who had lived through a propane plant explosion in 2008.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai
item.media:description: Drake performing in London last year. He posted two images on social media of an explosion that startled residents near Downsview Park in Toronto.
